Event/Battle: Transition to Iron Age (Hallstatt B phase)
Description: Elite burials in tumuli (barrows) begin, featuring iron swords, bronze vessels, jewelry, and chariots. These graves indicate hierarchical social structures13.

Event/Battle: Hallstatt C phase: Rise of elite burials
Description: Fortified hilltop settlements become common. Burial practices include wagon graves with elaborate goods, such as the Vix burial (500 BCE) in France137.

Event/Battle: Hallstatt D phase: Expansion of fortified settlements
Description: Stone sculptures such as the Hirschlanden warrior emerge, depicting ithyphallic male figures wearing torcs. These suggest religious or ceremonial significance7.

Event/Battle: Development of monumental sculptures
Description: This richly furnished burial includes a wagon grave, gold ornaments, and ceremonial drinking vessels37.

Year (BCE/CE): This timeline covers the key developments in Britain (which would later become England) during the period from 600 BC to 300 BIt's important to note that specific historical records for this period are limited, and much of our understanding comes from archaeological evidence. The events listed reflect the gradual development of Celtic culture, technological advancements, and societal changes in Britain during this time18.
